summary: Re-linked several `melee/vi` visual/cutscene objects by converting their assembly dumps from absolute-address style into relocatable object-style assembly, updating split boundaries, build classification, and nearby headers/includes. The PR split `vi.c` out of the tail of `tydisplay.c`, marked previously `Unknown` vi files as `NonMatching`, removed duplicate source object entries from `obj_files.mk`, and standardized small vi C wrappers around `GET_JOBJ`. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent beyond the re-link and mechanical cleanup is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Converted six vi assembly files (`vi.s`, `vi0402.s`, `vi0601.s`, `vi1201v1.s`, `vi1201v2.s`, `vi1202.s`) from absolute-address `.section`/`.global` dumps into re-linked object-style assembly with `.file`, `.text`, `.data`, `.balign`, `.fn`/`.endfn`, and `.obj`/`.endobj` annotations.
Adjusted assembly comments and labels to object-relative offsets such as `00000000` and local labels such as `.L_00000050` instead of ROM-address labels like `.L_8031C9EC`.

Rewrote data in `asm/melee/vi/vi.s` from `.asciz` strings to explicit `.4byte` words while preserving the filename table ending at `un_803FFFA8`; the excerpt includes `PlCaDViWaitAJ.dat` through `PlGnDViWaitAJ.dat` and preserves the odd casing in `PlMsDViWaitAj.dat`.

Updated `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `melee/ty/tydisplay.c` ends earlier at `.text` `0x8031C99C` and `.data` `0x803FFDA0`, then added a new `melee/vi/vi.c` split covering `.text` `0x8031C99C-0x8031CB00` and `.data` `0x803FFDA0-0x80400010`.

Changed `configure.py` object classifications from `Unknown` to `NonMatching` for `melee/vi/vi.c`, `vi0402.c`, `vi0601.c`, `vi1201v1.c`, `vi1201v2.c`, and `vi1202.c`; `melee/un_2FC9.c` was also changed from `Unknown` to `NonMatching`.

Removed source C object entries for `vi0402.c`, `vi0601.c`, `vi1201v1.c`, `vi1201v2.c`, and `vi1202.c` from `obj_files.mk`, leaving the corresponding `asm/melee/vi/*.s.o` entries in the text object list.

Standardized vi header guards from names like `_vi1202_h_` to address-based guards such as `GALE01_32110C`, removed direct `<platform.h>` and `<sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h>` includes from these headers, and kept `<baselib/forward.h>` for forward declarations.
Added address comments to vi prototypes, e.g. `/* 32110C */ void un_8032110C(HSD_GObj*);`.
Changed vi C files to include `<baselib/jobj.h>` instead of `<sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h>` and replaced raw `(HSD_JObj*) gobj->hsd_obj` casts with `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` before calling `HSD_JObjAnimAll`.
Marked `gm/forward.h` as an exported include from `src/melee/gm/types.h` using `// IWYU pragma: export`, then removed a redundant direct `gm/forward.h` include from `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c`.
smart_moves: Kept the relink mechanical and synchronized across assembly, splits, configure metadata, and object lists, which reduces the risk of link-order or duplicate-object regressions.
Used address-based header guards and prototype address comments, making partially named `un_` vi functions easier to correlate with map addresses during later decompilation.
Preserved exact data bytes in assembly via `.4byte` object definitions, which is safer than relying on assembler string directives when padding, alignment, or capitalization must match exactly.
Switched C helper code to `GET_JOBJ(gobj)`, reusing the project’s typed accessor instead of open-coded `hsd_obj` casts.
Used `baselib/forward.h` in headers and moved heavier baselib includes into C files, keeping headers lighter and more stable for include-what-you-use cleanup.
Added `IWYU pragma: export` to `gm/types.h` rather than re-adding `gm/forward.h` everywhere downstream.
decomp_lessons: When re-linking an assembly object, update all three build views together: the assembly file format, `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, and object classification/link lists.
If a source file is still `NonMatching`, ensure the build does not link both the source object and the hand-written/relinked assembly object for the same text range.
Splitting a region out of a neighboring file requires shrinking the previous file’s `.text`/`.data` ranges before adding the new split entry.
For not-yet-decompiled code, address-based header guards like `GALE01_31D6E4` and prototype comments preserve address knowledge without inventing semantic names.
Prefer project access macros such as `GET_JOBJ` over raw struct member casts when touching small nonmatching C wrappers; it improves type clarity without changing behavior.
Header cleanup should favor forward declarations in headers and concrete baselib includes in C files unless the full type is required.
Assembly data that looks like strings may still be safer as explicit words during relink work, especially when exact padding and byte layout matter.
naming_conventions: Function prototypes in vi headers use leading address comments such as `/* 31E6EC */` and `/* 321154 */`.
Re-linked assembly uses `.fn symbol, global` / `.endfn symbol` for functions and `.obj symbol, global` / `.endobj symbol` for data.
New split entry follows source path naming: `melee/vi/vi.c:` in `config/GALE01/splits.txt`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted absolute section declarations like `.section .text # 0x8031C99C - 0x8031CAFC` to object-local `.text` with an explicit object range comment.
Converted absolute instruction comments to object-relative comments while keeping the second displayed offset column.
Changed calls in relinked assembly to relocation-friendly placeholder encodings in the diff, e.g. `48 00 00 01` for external `bl` targets.
Replaced ROM-address local labels with object-local labels such as `.L_00000154`.
Wrapped each function in `.fn`/`.endfn` and each data symbol in `.obj`/`.endobj` to make the assembly match the project’s relinked object style.
Used `.balign 4` for text and `.balign 8` for data where shown.
Encoded string data as `.4byte` chunks to lock down byte-for-byte layout in `.data`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Because there is no human discussion, no reviewer intent can be attributed beyond the merged diff itself.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or match `melee/vi/vi.c` after this relink.
Search for other relink PRs that convert assembly to `.fn`/`.obj` style and update `splits.txt`.
Check whether `melee/un_2FC9.c` was intentionally changed to `NonMatching` as part of this relink or as nearby build cleanup.
Look for subsequent vi header renames that replace `un_8031*` symbols with semantic names.
Search for additional GET_JOBJ migration PRs touching baselib gobj/jobj wrappers.
